semantics: Each registered global receives one stable nonzero process-wide 32-bit identifier, and identifiers are never recycled.
Independent contexts and sibling realms receive distinct identifiers, including during parallel creation.
A live registered identifier resolves to its borrowed global context; zero and unknown identifiers resolve to null.
Explicit removal makes the identifier immediately unresolvable without changing the global's stable identifier.
Removal is context-isolated and idempotent for zero, unknown, and already-retired identifiers.
Every C-API context destruction path unregisters its identifier before freeing context-owned state.
The registry and identifier allocator are serialized across host threads.
The exact Home-profile library compiles, links, and runs a consumer covering explicit removal, natural teardown, sibling isolation, and parallel lifetimes.
